mnemonics: Think STAR: Facts are the STARS, Dimensions are the context.
keyPoints: Separate data into Fact Tables (metrics/measures) and Dimension Tables (descriptive context).
Use surrogate keys in dimension tables to handle changes over time (slowly changing dimensions).
Design for query performance and ease of use, favoring denormalized 'star schemas' over normalized models.
commonMistakes: Mixing facts and dimensions in the same table, violating the star schema principles.
Using natural keys instead of surrogate keys, leading to issues with historical data tracking.
Over-normalizing dimension tables, which harms query performance and simplicity for end-users.
